Conformal Conic)\n- `dataRepresentationTemplateNumber`: 3 = DRT=3 (Complex packing with spatial differencing)\n- `gridType`: Projection type (e.g., \"lambert\")\n- `Nx`, `Ny`: Grid dimensions\n- Note icc and pgc have been\n23\tfound to be incompatible with the Jasper library and are not allowed.\n24\t\n25\t       sh/bash:    export CC=gcc\n26\t       csh/tcsh:   setenv CC gcc\n27\t\n28\tCompile the wgrib2 using gnu make\n29\t\n30\t       linux:      make\n31\t       MacOS:      gmake\n32\t       Unix:       ???    ask system administrators\n33\t\n34\tTry executing wgrib2\n35\t\n36\t      wgrib2/wgrib2 -config\n37\t\n38\tCopy wgrib2 to SOMEPLACE\n39\t\n40\t      cygwin/linux/unix: cp wgrib2/wgrib2 SOMEPLACE\n41\t\n42\t\n43\t                          Not So Quick Directions\n44\t                          So you want some options\n45\t\n46\t\n47\tEdit the file \"makefile\" and select the desired options:\n48\t\n49\t# netcdf3: write netcdf files with netcdf-3 library\n50\t# netcdf4: write netcdf files with netcdf-4 library\n51\t# regex: regular expression package used by (match,not), POSIX-2\n52\t# tigge: enable -tigge option for tigge names\n53\t# mysql: write to mysql files\n54\t# ipolates: fortran interpolation library\n55\t# udf: user defined functions\n56\t# openmp: multicore support using OpenMP\n57\t# proj4: use proj4 for geolocation\n58\t# wmo_validation: used for testing new templates\n59\t# fortran_api:  fortran api for callable wgrib2, requires a fortran 2003\n60\t#               feature, subroutines can allocated/deallocated arrays\n61\t# disable_timezone: some OS do not handle time zones in POSIX maner\n62\t#\n63\t# the flags are stored in wgrib2/config.h\n64\t#\n65\t# Warning do not set both USE_NETCDF3 and USE_NETCDF4 to one\n66\t\n67\tThese are the default options\n68\t\n69\tUSE_NETCDF3=1\n70\tUSE_NETCDF4=0\n71\tUSE_REGEX=1\n72\tUSE_TIGGE=1\n73\tUSE_MYSQL=0\n74\tUSE_IPOLATES=1\n75\tUSE_UDF=0\n76\tUSE_OPENMP=1\n77\tUSE_PROJ4=0\n78\tUSE_WMO_VALIDATION=0\n79\tDISABLE_TIMEZONE=0\n80\tMAKE_FTN_API=1\n81\t\n82\tUSE_G2CLIB=0\n83\tUSE_PNG=1\n84\tUSE_JASPER=1\n85\t\n86\tCOMMENTS:\n87\t\n88\t1) Do not set both USE_NETCDF3 and USE_NETCDF4 to one\n89\t2) Some options require a compatible fortran compiler (usually gfortran).\n90\t   You need to set the environment variable FC before running make\n91\t\n92\t       sh/bash:    export FC=gfortran\n93\t       csh/tcsh:   setenv FC gfortran\n94\t\n95\t3) On some machines, you will have to define the C compiler\n96\t   and C preprocessor.  On the NCEP's AIX, I have to define\n97\t   them by\n98\t\n99\t\texport CC=/usr/vacpp/bin/xlc_r\n100\t\texport CPP=/usr/bin/cpp\n101\t\n102\t\n103\t                          Netcdf\n104\t\n105\tThe makefile is set up to use either netcdf v3 or netcdf v4.  Netcdf-4\n106\tmakes wgrib2 much bigger, so don't install it unless you really have to.\n107\tThe source code for netcdf-4 (and necessary hdf5) is also very big\n108\tand will have to be downloaded separately.  The makefile will\n109\tprompt you for the downloads.  You also have to set the fortran\n110\tcompiler. See (2).\n111\t\n112\tThe netcdf-3 library is included with this distribution.  The library is\n113\tcompiled in enable-C-only mode which is not be that useful for\n114\tfortran code.  Don't use try to link other codes to this library.\n115\t\n116\t                              MYSQL\n117\t\n118\tInstall MySQL requires the appropriate headers and libraries to be installed.\n119\tThe makefile may have to be modified to reflect the locations of the headers\n120\tand libraries.\n121\t\n122\t\n123\t                  IPOLATES, interpolation library\n124\t\n125\tIPOLATES is the interpolation library used by the -new_grid options.\n126\tIn order to use this library, you must have a f90/f95/f2003 fortran compiler\n127\tenabled.  See (2).  You will have to change the source code if you are \n128\tnot using g95, gfortran, openf95 or xlf.\n129\t\n130\t                              USE_UDF\n131\t\n132\tSet to zero in 99.999% of the times. \n133\t\n134\t                             USE_PROJ4\n135\t\n136\tWgrib2 can use Proj4 as its geolocation library.  The default geolocation \n137\tlibrary, gctpc, is faster.  Proj4 is most used to compare check the\n138\tresults from gctpc.\n139\t\n140\t\n141\t                        USE_WMO_VALIDATION\n142\t\n143\tHas been used for validation of proposed WMO extentions.\n144\t\n145\t                       DISABLE_TIMEZONE\n146\t\n147\tSet to zero if most cases.  For one non-POSIX system, the time zones\n148\thad to be disabled in order for a certain option to work correctly.\n149\t\n150\t\n151\t                             OpenMP\n152\t\n153\tOpenMP allows wgrib2 to run faster by using threading the code and\n154\trunning on multiple cores.  When you enable OpenMP, your C compiler must \n155\tsupport OpenMP v3.0 or higher.  \n156\t\n157\t\n158\t                          MAKE_FTN_API\n159\t\n160\tThe MAKE_FTN_API builds the wgrib2 library and the C and fortran API.\n161\tThis enables C and Fortran codes to call wgrib2 as a subroutine. This\n162\talso requires a fortran compiler.\n163\t\n164\t\n165\t                            USE_G2CLIB\n166\t\n167\tThe internal grib decoders are better than g2clib so there is\n168\tlittle reason to link in g2clib.  However, I've gotten complaints\n169\tthat wgrib2 decoded this field incorrectly.  My reply is that g2clib\n170\tgot the same results.\n171\t\n172\t\n173\t                              USE_PNG\n174\t\n175\tIf set to zero, the png library is not linked in and png-compressed\n176\tgrib message cannot be read.  When was the last time you saw a\n177\tpng-compressed grib message?  Must be set to one if g2clib is\n178\tlinked int.\n179\t\n180\t                            USE_JASPER\n181\t\n182\tIf set to zero, the jasper library used for encoding and decoding\n183\tjpeg2000 is not linked in.  Since jpeg2000-compressed files are\n184\tvery common, USE_JASPER should be set to one.  The only exception\n185\tis when you are cross-compiling wgrib2.  Using autoconfig scripts\n186\t(Proj4, png, jasper) can be a challenge.  Of course, you would\n187\thave restrict yourself to simple, complex or ieee packed grib.\n188\t\n189\t\n190\t                     INSTALLING on Cygwin and Windows\n191\t\n192\tThe code including the IPOLATES option compiles in the cygwin development \n193\tsystem.  You'll have to install extra packages such as the C, gfortran \n194\tcompilers and various scripting utilities. \n195\t\n196\t    Before you compile, you set the default compilers.\n197\t\n198\t    export CC=gcc\n199\t    export FC=gfortran\n200\t    make\n201\t\n202\t                        INSTALLING on FreeBSD\n203\t\n204\tSet appropriate CC and FC environment variables and try make. wgrib2 v1.9.8\n205\twas compiled with clang and gfortran.\n206\t\n207\t\n208\t                        INSTALLING on Apple OS-X\n209\t\n210\tSee Grace Peng's blog.\n211\t\n212\t\n213\t                        INSTALLING on NCEP's AIX\n214\t\n215\tDefine default C, C preprocessor and fortran\n216\t\n217\t   export CC=/usr/vacpp/bin/xlc_r\n218\t   export CPP=/usr/bin/cpp\n219\t   export FC=xlf_r\n220\t   gmake\n221\t\n222\t\n223\t\t\t\tStatic vs Dynamic Libraries\n224\t\n225\tThe various libraries can be created in both dynamic and static versions.  \n226\tI've tried to make the add-on libraries static.  \n227\t\n228\t                           Testing Package\n229\t\n230\tYes it does exist.\n231\t\n232\t                             Problems\n233\t\n234\tPerfection? Note icc and pgc have been\nfound to be incompatible with the Jasper library and are not allowed.\n\n       sh/bash:    export CC=gcc\n       csh/tcsh:   setenv CC gcc\n\nCompile the wgrib2 using gnu make\n\n       linux:      make\n       MacOS:      gmake\n       Unix:       ???    ask system administrators\n\nTry executing wgrib2\n\n      wgrib2/wgrib2 -config\n\nCopy wgrib2 to SOMEPLACE\n\n      cygwin/linux/unix: cp wgrib2/wgrib2 SOMEPLACE\n\n\n                          Not So Quick Directions\n                          So you want some options\n\n\nEdit the file \"makefile\" and select the desired options:\n\n# netcdf3: write netcdf files with netcdf-3 library\n# netcdf4: write netcdf files with netcdf-4 library\n# regex: regular expression package used by (match,not), POSIX-2\n# tigge: enable -tigge option for tigge names\n# mysql: write to mysql files\n# ipolates: fortran interpolation library\n# udf: user defined functions\n# openmp: multicore support using OpenMP\n# proj4: use proj4 for geolocation\n# wmo_validation: used for testing new templates\n# fortran_api:  fortran api for callable wgrib2, requires a fortran 2003\n#               feature, subroutines can allocated/deallocated arrays\n# disable_timezone: some OS do not handle time zones in POSIX maner\n#\n# the flags are stored in wgrib2/config.h\n#\n# Warning do not set both USE_NETCDF3 and USE_NETCDF4 to one\n\nThese are the default options\n\nUSE_NETCDF3=1\nUSE_NETCDF4=0\nUSE_REGEX=1\nUSE_TIGGE=1\nUSE_MYSQL=0\nUSE_IPOLATES=1\nUSE_UDF=0\nUSE_OPENMP=1\nUSE_PROJ4=0\nUSE_WMO_VALIDATION=0\nDISABLE_TIMEZONE=0\nMAKE_FTN_API=1\n\nUSE_G2CLIB=0\nUSE_PNG=1\nUSE_JASPER=1\n\nCOMMENTS:\n\n1) Do not set both USE_NETCDF3 and USE_NETCDF4 to one\n2) Some options require a compatible fortran compiler (usually gfortran).\n   You need to set the environment variable FC before running make\n\n       sh/bash:    export FC=gfortran\n       csh/tcsh:   setenv FC gfortran\n\n3) On some machines, you will have to define the C compiler\n   and C preprocessor.  On the NCEP's AIX, I have to define\n   them by\n\n\texport CC=/usr/vacpp/bin/xlc_r\n\texport CPP=/usr/bin/cpp\n\n\n                          Netcdf\n\nThe makefile is set up to use either netcdf v3 or netcdf v4.  Netcdf-4\nmakes wgrib2 much bigger, so don't install it unless you really have to.\nThe source code for netcdf-4 (and necessary hdf5) is also very big\nand will have to be downloaded separately.  The makefile will\nprompt you for the downloads.  You also have to set the fortran\ncompiler. See (2).\n\nThe netcdf-3 library is included with this distribution.  The library is\ncompiled in enable-C-only mode which is not be that useful for\nfortran code.  Don't use try to link other codes to this library.\n\n                              MYSQL\n\nInstall MySQL requires the appropriate headers and libraries to be installed.\nThe makefile may have to be modified to reflect the locations of the headers\nand libraries.\n\n\n                  IPOLATES, interpolation library\n\nIPOLATES is the interpolation library used by the -new_grid options.\nIn order to use this library, you must have a f90/f95/f2003 fortran compiler\nenabled.  See (2).  You will have to change the source code if you are \nnot using g95, gfortran, openf95 or xlf.\n\n                              USE_UDF\n\nSet to zero in 99.999% of the times. \n\n                             USE_PROJ4\n\nWgrib2 can use Proj4 as its geolocation library.  The default geolocation \nlibrary, gctpc, is faster.  Proj4 is most used to compare check the\nresults from gctpc.\n\n\n                        USE_WMO_VALIDATION\n\nHas been used for validation of proposed WMO extentions.\n\n                       DISABLE_TIMEZONE\n\nSet to zero if most cases.  For one non-POSIX system, the time zones\nhad to be disabled in order for a certain option to work correctly.\n\n\n                             OpenMP\n\nOpenMP allows wgrib2 to run faster by using threading the code and\nrunning on multiple cores.  When you enable OpenMP, your C compiler must \nsupport OpenMP v3.0 or higher.  \n\n\n                          MAKE_FTN_API\n\nThe MAKE_FTN_API builds the wgrib2 library and the C and fortran API.\nThis enables C and Fortran codes to call wgrib2 as a subroutine. This\nalso requires a fortran compiler.\n\n\n                            USE_G2CLIB\n\nThe internal grib decoders are better than g2clib so there is\nlittle reason to link in g2clib.  However, I've gotten complaints\nthat wgrib2 decoded this field incorrectly.  My reply is that g2clib\ngot the same results.\n\n\n                              USE_PNG\n\nIf set to zero, the png library is not linked in and png-compressed\ngrib message cannot be read.  When was the last time you saw a\npng-compressed grib message?  Must be set to one if g2clib is\nlinked int.\n\n                            USE_JASPER\n\nIf set to zero, the jasper library used for encoding and decoding\njpeg2000 is not linked in.  Since jpeg2000-compressed files are\nvery common, USE_JASPER should be set to one.  The only exception\nis when you are cross-compiling wgrib2.  Using autoconfig scripts\n(Proj4, png, jasper) can be a challenge.  Of course, you would\nhave restrict yourself to simple, complex or ieee packed grib.\n\n\n                     INSTALLING on Cygwin and Windows\n\nThe code including the IPOLATES option compiles in the cygwin development \nsystem.  You'll have to install extra packages such as the C, gfortran \ncompilers and various scripting utilities. \n\n    Before you compile, you set the default compilers.\n\n    export CC=gcc\n    export FC=gfortran\n    make\n\n                        INSTALLING on FreeBSD\n\nSet appropriate CC and FC environment variables and try make. wgrib2 v1.9.8\nwas compiled with clang and gfortran.\n\n\n                        INSTALLING on Apple OS-X\n\nSee Grace Peng's blog.\n\n\n                        INSTALLING on NCEP's AIX\n\nDefine default C, C preprocessor and fortran\n\n   export CC=/usr/vacpp/bin/xlc_r\n   export CPP=/usr/bin/cpp\n   export FC=xlf_r\n   gmake\n\n\n\t\t\tStatic vs Dynamic Libraries\n\nThe various libraries can be created in both dynamic and static versions.  \nI've tried to make the add-on libraries static.  \n\n                           Testing Package\n\nYes it does exist.\n\n                             Problems\n\nPerfection?  Not here.\n\n\n","numLines":237,"startLine":1,"totalLines":237}}}
